target.title: fix(agents): prefer identity.name over agent slot id in Runtime prompt line (fixes #81269)
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: The Runtime line in the system prompt renders `agent=<slotId>` even when a distinct `identity.name` is configured, causing models to identify themselves by the slot id instead of their configured persona
- **Root Cause**: `buildRuntimeLine()` in `src/agents/system-prompt.ts` only reads `runtimeInfo.agentId` (the slot id) and `buildSystemPromptParams()` in `src/agents/system-prompt-params.ts` does not resolve `identity.name` from the agent config
- **Fix**: Resolve `identity.name` via `resolveAgentConfig()` in `buildSystemPromptParams`, inject it as `identityName` in `runtimeInfo`, and prefer `identityName` over `agentId` when rendering the Runtime line
- **What changed**:
  - `src/agents/system-prompt-params.ts` — resolve `identity.name` from agent config and add `identityName` to `runtimeInfo`
  - `src/agents/system-prompt.ts` — accept `identityName` in `runtimeInfo` types and prefer it over `agentId` in `buildRuntimeLine`
  - `src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts` — add two test cases: identityName preferred, and agentId fallback
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - No changes to `sessionKey`, `sessionId`, or any other Runtime line field
  - No changes to agent slot routing, session resolution, or identity workspace files
  - No changes to system prompt structure outside the Runtime line

### Reproduction

1. Configure an agent slot with `id: "main"` and `identity: { name: "Runt" }`
2. Start the gateway and send a message
3. **Before this PR**: The Runtime line in the system prompt shows `agent=main`, causing the model to identify as "main" despite IDENTITY.md saying "Runt"
4. **After this PR**: The Runtime line shows `agent=Runt`, matching the configured identity

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed (#81269)**: The system prompt Runtime line injects the agent slot id (`agentId`) instead of the configured `identity.name`, causing identity drift when a deployment uses a generic slot id but a distinct persona name. This fix prefers `identity.name` over `agentId` when both are available.

**Real environment tested**: Linux, Node 22 — isolated function-level validation of `buildRuntimeLine` and `buildAgentSystemPrompt`

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`

**Evidence after fix**:
```text
Before fix (agentId only, main branch behavior):
Runtime: agent=main | host=host | os=linux (x64) | node=v22 | model=anthropic/claude | channel=telegram | capabilities=none | thinking=off

After fix (identityName preferred, this PR):
Runtime: agent=Runt | host=host | os=linux (x64) | node=v22 | model=anthropic/claude | channel=telegram | capabilities=none | thinking=off
```

**Observed result after fix**: `buildRuntimeLine` returns `agent=Runt` when `identityName: "Runt"` is set alongside `agentId: "main"`, and falls back to `agent=main` when only `agentId` is set. The new "prefers identityName over agentId" test case passes on the patched branch (renders `agent=Runt`) and fails on origin/main (renders `agent=main`). The backward-compatibility fallback case also passes. The full end-to-end pipeline (config → `buildSystemPromptParams` → `buildAgentSystemPrompt` → Runtime line) was verified with a real agent config containing `identity: { name: "Runt" }` on slot `"main"`, producing the expected `agent=Runt` in the system prompt output.

**What was not tested**: Live gateway round-trip with a real channel was not driven; the fix is validated at the `buildRuntimeLine` function level via unit tests that cover both the identityName-preferred and agentId-fallback paths.

**Repro confirmation**: The new test case "prefers identityName over agentId in runtime line" fails on origin/main (buildRuntimeLine ignores identityName and renders agent=main) and passes after the patch (buildRuntimeLine renders agent=Runt).

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: If `resolveAgentConfig()` returns `undefined` for an agent id that doesn't exist in config, `identityName` will be `undefined` and the Runtime line falls back to `agentId` — identical to current behavior
  **Mitigation**: The fix uses `identityName ?? agentId` with optional chaining throughout; `identityName` is only set when `agentConfig?.identity?.name` is a non-empty string
- **Risk**: `resolveAgentConfig` is already imported and used by `system-prompt-config.ts` in the same call path; this fix adds one additional call per system prompt build
  **Mitigation**: `resolveAgentConfig` is a synchronous config lookup (no I/O); the overhead is negligible

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] agents (system prompt rendering)
- [x] config (identity resolution)

### Regression Test Plan

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/system-prompt-params.test.ts`

### Review Findings Addressed

- **[P1] Add redacted real setup proof**: Added end-to-end system prompt generation with a configured `identity: { name: "Runt" }` on agent slot `"main"`, confirming the Runtime line renders `agent=Runt` through the full config → `buildSystemPromptParams` → `buildAgentSystemPrompt` pipeline.
- **[P1] Add `buildSystemPromptParams` regression test**: Added two tests — "threads identity.name into runtimeInfo when configured" and "leaves identityName undefined when identity.name is not configured" — to verify the config-plumbing path.

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #81269
target.author: zenglingbiao
